data.title: Warren Buffett holds enough cash to buy 476 companies in the S&P 500 at current values
data.description:
Warren Buffett has stacked up so much cash at Berkshire Hathaway, he could straight-up buy the bottom 476 companies in the S&P 500 right now. His cash pile is sitting at $334 billion, and that’s enough to swallow 95% of the index. The S&P 500, which covers about 80% of all U. S. public equities by […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/warren-buffett-476-companies-in-the-sp-500/

data.title: Ethena Pulls Out of Germany Amid Regulator's USDe Stablecoin Scrutiny
data.description:

data.title: White House: President Trump has not yet made a decision on raising corporate tax rates
data.description: White House: We currently have more than 15 trade deals under active review. Trump hopes to sign the trade deals himself. President Trump has not made a decision on raising corporate tax rates.

data.title: The U.S. Treasury Department discusses whether to relax U.S. debt capital regulations
data.description: U.S. Treasury Undersecretary Michael Faulkender said officials are discussing a possible rule change for banks. As U.S. Treasuries fell last week, attention to the "supplementary leverage ratio" (SLR) regulation has skyrocketed. The biggest drop in U.S. Treasuries in more than two decades has raised concerns about a market crash similar to the March 2020 crash. Any rule change would still require approval from the Federal Reserve and other regulators, although the Treasury secretary is chair of the regulatory committee responsible for U.S. financial stability. "We are looking into it and have already started discussions," Faulkender said at an event.

data.title: The US SEC rejected Musk's DOGE unit's request to obtain certain institutional data
data.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has rejected a request from Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) for certain agency data.

data.title: Republicans reportedly considering 40% tax rate on millionaires to offset tax cuts
data.description: Republicans in the White House, Senate and House of Representatives are drafting analyses of how best to set new tax brackets for the wealthiest Americans, a sign that President Donald Trump's party is seriously considering raising taxes on the wealthy. The House proposal would set a 40% tax rate on taxpayers making $1 million or more, according to people familiar with the matter. The Senate and Trump administration economic policy advisers are also studying the idea, the sources said. A White House official said Trump is open to creating a new top tax bracket. But the official stressed that the higher tax rate should start well above $1 million.
